prerequisites: An active Freightview account with API access enabled. API access is a provisioned integration feature; contact Freightview to enable it for your account if you do not already have it.
A Client ID (config field `client_id`), generated in the Freightview app under Settings > API integration.
A Client Secret (config field `client_secret`), generated alongside the Client ID under Settings > API integration.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials
authMethods.summary: Freightview's v2.0 API uses the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant. You authenticate by POSTing your `client_id` and `client_secret` (with `grant_type=client_credentials`) to the token endpoint `https://api.freightview.com/v2.0/auth/token`, which returns an `access_token`. That token is then sent as a Bearer token on API requests and is valid for about one day before it must be refreshed. These map to the connector's two secret fields, `client_id` and `client_secret`.

setupSteps.title: Enable API access on your Freightview account
setupSteps.body: The Freightview API is an integration feature that must be enabled for your account. If you do not already see the API integration option, contact Freightview via the integration page at https://www.freightview.com/integration to have API access provisioned.

setupSteps.title: Generate your Client ID and Client Secret
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Freightview account and navigate to Settings > API integration. Open the V2 API window, then copy the Client ID and the Client Secret. These are the credentials Freightview's v2.0 API expects.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied values into the connector's required fields: `client_id` (Client ID) and `client_secret` (Client Secret). The connector exchanges these for an access token at https://api.freightview.com/v2.0/auth/token using the client_credentials grant and uses the returned token as a Bearer token for all API calls.

sources.title: Airbyte source-freightview connector manifest (token endpoint, client_credentials grant, Bearer auth)
